title.en: Phoenix: Safe GitHub Issue Resolution via Multi-Agent LLMs
title.zh: Phoenix：基于多智能体LLM的安全GitHub问题解决

abstract: We present Phoenix, a multi-agent LLM system that resolves GitHub issues from triage through pull-request creation, combining seven layered safety controls with a baseline-aware test evaluation strategy. Phoenix decomposes the work across six specialized agents. Planner, reproducer, coder, tester, failure analyst and Pull Request (PR) agent, all coordinated by a label-based GitHub webhook state machine. Every change is checked against a baseline test run before a pull request is opened. On a 24-instance slice of SWE-bench Lite. run on the production webhook path, Phoenix oracle-resolves 75% of instances with no pass-to-pass regressions on successful runs; this curated slice is not directly comparable to full-split leaderboard results, and we discuss the limits of the comparison. A complementary pilot on 42 real issues across 14 repositories yields 100% correctness preservation (CP; mean 122s on the hard tier). Manual inspection shows that about half of the resulting pull requests are well-targeted fixes. The other half place code at incorrect paths, a planner localization limitation we are addressing with retrieval. We also report the deployment failure modes (WAF filtering, token expiry, permission boundaries, flaky CI) that motivated each safety mechanism.

relevance.en: The paper's baseline-aware test strategy directly addresses overfitting by preventing any regression, confirming a practical validation approach for LLM-based APR, but its finding that half of patches are placed at incorrect paths explicitly challenges current LLM fault localization, aligning with your interest in localization and correctness.
relevance.zh: 该论文的基线感知测试策略通过防止任何回归直接解决了过拟合问题，证实了基于LLM的自动程序修复（APR）的一种实用验证方法，但其发现一半补丁被放置在错误路径上，这明确挑战了当前的LLM故障定位，与你对定位和正确性的兴趣一致。

figures.label: Figure 2

caption.en:
Phoenix agent pipeline. After planning, the Reproducer
(when enabled) attempts to add a failing test that demonstrates
the issue on the base branch; implementation and testing fol-
low, with Failure Analyst feedback on test failure. Reproducer
failure is non-blocking. On retry exhaustion the issue is labeled
ai:failed.
caption.zh: Phoenix智能体流水线。规划后，Reproducer（若启用）尝试添加一个失败的测试以在基础分支上演示问题；随后进行实现和测试，Failure Analyst对测试失败提供反馈。Reproducer失败不阻塞流程。重试耗尽后，问题被标记为ai:failed。

figures.reference_text: Phoenix orchestrates six specialized LLM agents in a closed-loop pipeline, coordinated through a central Orches- trator. Fig. 1 provides the broad overview of the pipeline, and Fig 2 illustrates the end-to-end agentic flow.

figures.label: Figure 3

caption.en:
Baseline-aware test evaluation. Phoenix stashes its
changes and runs the suite on the unmodified branch (step
2) to collect baseline failures B, then restores changes and
re-runs (step 4) to collect post-change failures P. Correctness
is preserved iff P \ B = ∅: no previously passing test now
fails.
caption.zh: 基线感知测试评估。Phoenix暂存其更改，在未修改分支上运行测试套件（步骤2）以收集基线失败B，然后恢复更改并重新运行（步骤4）以收集更改后失败P。正确性保持当且仅当P \ B = ∅：没有先前通过的测试现在失败。

figures.reference_text: If the baseline already fails and Phoenix introduced no new failures, the run is classified as correctness preserved. This matters when evaluating Phoenix on repositories with broken CI pipelines. Fig. 3 illustrates the strategy. 5) Failure Analyst Agent: When the Tester detects new failures, the Failure Analyst receives the full test output, the failure set, and a summary of the run. It produces structured

figures.label: Figure 4

caption.en:
GitHub label state machine. Labels serve as the
persistent state store; transitions are atomic and mutually
exclusive. The ai:revise loop enables iterative human-in-
the-loop refinement. ai:failed is a terminal failure state;
ai:review is the handoff to human review.
caption.zh: GitHub标签状态机。标签作为持久状态存储；转换是原子且互斥的。ai:revise循环支持迭代的人工参与改进。ai:failed是终端失败状态；ai:review是交接给人工审查。

figures.reference_text: Phoenix maintains a per-issue state using GitHub labels as the persistent state store, without requiring a local database. Fig. 4 shows the five-state machine. Label transitions are atomic. The new label replaces all AI- state labels. This ensures at most one active state per issue. A loop-prevention filter discards label events originating from the application installation itself. A per-installation lock serializes concurrent issues on the shared clone; separate repositories run in parallel.

caption.zh: Phoenix智能体流水线。规划后，Reproducer（若启用）尝试添加一个失败的测试以在基础分支上演示问题；随后进行实现和测试，Failure Analyst对测试失败提供反馈。Reproducer失败不阻塞流程。重试耗尽后，问题被标记为ai:failed。

figure.reference_text: Phoenix orchestrates six specialized LLM agents in a closed-loop pipeline, coordinated through a central Orches- trator. Fig. 1 provides the broad overview of the pipeline, and Fig 2 illustrates the end-to-end agentic flow.

brief_summary.en:
Automated bug fixing in real-world GitHub repositories is risky: a patch that passes tests might still break existing functionality or introduce subtle regressions. Prior systems either operate in controlled benchmarks or lack the safety mechanisms needed for production deployment. Phoenix addresses this gap with a multi-agent LLM system that resolves issues from triage to pull-request creation, combining six specialized agents (Planner, Reproducer, Coder, Tester, Failure Analyst, PR Agent) with seven layered safety controls—including baseline-aware regression testing that compares test results against the unmodified branch to guarantee no previously passing test fails.

On a curated 24-instance slice of SWE-bench Lite, Phoenix oracle-resolved 75% of instances with zero pass-to-pass regressions, and a pilot on 42 real issues across 14 repositories achieved 100% correctness preservation with a mean runtime of 122 seconds. However, manual inspection revealed that about half of the resulting pull requests placed code at incorrect file paths—a planner localization limitation the authors are addressing with retrieval-augmented generation. The baseline-aware test strategy effectively prevents overfitting to the test suite but does not catch semantic errors that don't cause test failures, highlighting a key trade-off between deployability and rigorous validation.
brief_summary.zh:
在实际的GitHub仓库中自动修复漏洞是有风险的：一个通过测试的补丁仍可能破坏现有功能或引入微妙的回归。之前的系统要么在受控基准上运行，要么缺乏生产部署所需的安全机制。Phoenix通过一个多智能体LLM系统弥合了这一差距，该系统从问题分类到拉取请求创建全程解决问题，结合了六个专门智能体（Planner, Reproducer, Coder, Tester, Failure Analyst, PR Agent）和七层安全控制——包括基线感知回归测试，该测试将测试结果与未修改分支进行比较，以保证任何先前通过的测试不会失败。

在SWE-bench Lite的一个精心挑选的24个实例子集上，Phoenix解决了75%的实例，且零通过到通过的回归，在一项涉及14个仓库的42个真实问题的试点中，实现了100%的正确性保持，平均运行时间为122秒。然而，人工检查发现，大约一半的拉取请求将代码放在了错误的文件路径上——这是规划器定位上的局限性，作者正通过检索增强生成来解决。基线感知测试策略有效防止了过拟合测试套件，但不能捕获不会导致测试失败的语义错误，凸显了可部署性与严格验证之间的关键权衡。

problem.en: The core problem is safely resolving real-world GitHub issues automatically without introducing regressions. Prior work on code generation and automated program repair often assumes clean, controlled environments (e.g., static bug datasets) and does not account for the complex pipeline from issue triage to pull request creation in production settings. There is no existing system that combines multi-agent LLM coordination, safety controls, and baseline-aware regression testing for GitHub issues. This gap is practically important because deploying LLMs to fix real issues risks breaking existing functionality, wasting developer time, and causing CI failures. Phoenix addresses the need for a deployable, safe system that can autonomously navigate GitHub's environment (including webhooks, labels, state machines) and guarantee no regression in tests.
problem.zh: 核心问题是在不引入回归的情况下安全地自动解决真实的GitHub问题。先前关于代码生成和自动程序修复的工作通常假设清洁、受控的环境（例如静态漏洞数据集），并未考虑从问题分类到生产环境中拉取请求创建的复杂流水线。现有系统没有结合多智能体LLM协调、安全控制和针对GitHub问题的基线感知回归测试。这一差距在实际中很重要，因为部署LLM修复真实问题可能会破坏现有功能，浪费开发人员时间，并导致CI失败。Phoenix满足了可部署、安全系统的需求，该系统能够自主导航GitHub的环境（包括webhooks、标签、状态机），并保证测试无回归。

solution.en: Phoenix's main contribution is a multi-agent LLM system that resolves GitHub issues from triage through pull-request creation with seven layered safety controls and a baseline-aware test evaluation strategy. The key insight is that by decomposing the work across six specialized agents (Planner, Reproducer, Coder, Tester, Failure Analyst, PR Agent) coordinated by a label-based GitHub webhook state machine, and by comparing test results against a baseline run on the unmodified branch, the system can guarantee correctness preservation—no previously passing test fails after a change. This differs from prior work that typically uses a single agent or lacks safety mechanisms for production deployment.
solution.zh: Phoenix的主要贡献是一个多智能体LLM系统，该系统通过七层安全控制和基线感知测试评估策略，从问题分类到拉取请求创建来解决问题。关键洞察在于，通过将工作分解为六个专门智能体（Planner, Reproducer, Coder, Tester, Failure Analyst, PR Agent），由基于标签的GitHub webhook状态机协调，并将测试结果与未修改分支上的基线运行进行比较，系统可以保证正确性保持——更改后没有先前通过的测试失败。这与先前通常使用单个智能体或缺乏生产部署安全机制的工作不同。

methodology.en: Phoenix operates as a multi-agent system coordinated by an Orchestrator that uses a GitHub webhook state machine (Figure 4). The pipeline has five main stages: (1) Planning by the Planner agent, which reads the issue and repository context (e.g., README, file tree) to propose a fix plan. (2) Reproducing (optional, enabled via label): the Reproducer agent attempts to add a failing test that demonstrates the issue on the base branch; failure is non-blocking. (3) Implementation: the Coder agent generates code changes based on the plan. (4) Testing: the Tester agent runs tests with baseline-aware evaluation (Figure 3): Phoenix stashes changes, runs the test suite on the unmodified branch to collect baseline failures B, restores changes, re-runs to collect post-change failures P, then checks correctness preservation (P \ B = ∅). (5) Failure Analysis: if new failures appear, the Failure Analyst agent analyzes the output and provides feedback for the Coder to revise. A retry loop allows up to a configurable number of revisions before labeling ai:failed. The PR Agent then creates a pull request. The state machine uses GitHub labels (e.g., ai:planning, ai:coding, ai:testing, ai:review, ai:failed) as persistent state store without a local database. Label transitions are atomic and mutually exclusive. A loop-prevention filter discards label events from the installation itself. The system includes seven safety controls: (1) webhook event filtering, (2) permission boundaries (GitHub App permissions scoped to write only on specific repos), (3) token expiry handling, (4) WAF (Web Application Firewall) protection, (5) flaky CI detection, (6) baseline-aware regression testing, and (7) human-in-the-loop review via ai:revise label. The deployment runs on a production webhook path using GitHub Actions runners.
methodology.zh: Phoenix作为一个多智能体系统运行，由使用GitHub webhook状态机的编排器协调（图4）。流水线有五个主要阶段：（1）规划阶段，由Planner智能体执行，读取问题与仓库上下文（如README、文件树）以提出修复计划。（2）复现阶段（可选，通过标签启用）：Reproducer智能体尝试添加一个失败的测试，以在基础分支上演示问题；失败不阻塞流程。（3）实现阶段：Coder智能体根据计划生成代码更改。（4）测试阶段：Tester智能体运行测试，采用基线感知评估（图3）：Phoenix暂存更改，在未修改分支上运行测试套件以收集基线失败B，恢复更改，重新运行以收集更改后失败P，然后检查正确性保持（P \ B = ∅）。（5）失败分析阶段：如果出现新的失败，Failure Analyst智能体分析输出并为Coder提供反馈以进行修订。重试循环允许最多可配置次数的修订，然后标记为ai:failed。然后PR Agent创建拉取请求。状态机使用GitHub标签（如ai:planning、ai:coding、ai:testing、ai:review、ai:failed）作为持久状态存储，无需本地数据库。标签转换是原子且互斥的。循环预防过滤器丢弃来自安装本身的标签事件。系统包含七项安全控制：（1）webhook事件过滤、（2）权限边界（GitHub应用的权限限定为仅对特定仓库写入）、（3）令牌过期处理、（4）WAF（Web应用防火墙）保护、（5）不稳定的CI检测、（6）基线感知回归测试，以及（7）通过ai:revise标签进行的人工参与审查。部署在GitHub Actions运行器上的生产webhook路径运行。

experiments.en: Phoenix was evaluated on two benchmarks. (1) A 24-instance slice of SWE-bench Lite, selected because those instances could be triggered via webhook (not CLI) on the production deployment. On this curated slice, Phoenix oracle-resolved 75% (18/24) of instances with no pass-to-pass regressions on successful runs. The authors caution that this slice is not directly comparable to full-split leaderboard results. (2) A complementary pilot on 42 real GitHub issues across 14 repositories, yielding 100% correctness preservation (CP) with a mean runtime of 122 seconds on the hard tier. Manual inspection of resulting pull requests showed that about half were well-targeted fixes, while the other half placed code at incorrect paths—a planner localization limitation. No baselines (e.g., other multi-agent systems or prior tools like RepoFix, CodeGen with retrieval) were compared against because the evaluation focuses on safety and deployability rather than raw resolution rate. Ablations were performed implicitly via the safety control stack (e.g., reproducing baseline failures to detect flaky CI).
experiments.zh: Phoenix在两个基准上进行了评估。（1）SWE-bench Lite的一个24实例子集，选择这些实例是因为它们可以通过webhook（而非CLI）在生产部署中触发。在这个精心挑选的子集上，Phoenix oracle解决了75%（18/24）的实例，且成功运行时没有通过到通过的回归。作者提醒，该子集不能直接与完整分片的排行榜结果比较。（2）在14个仓库的42个真实GitHub问题上进行了补充试点，在困难层级上实现了100%的正确性保持（CP），平均运行时间为122秒。对生成的拉取请求的人工检查显示，约一半是定位准确的修复，而另一半将代码放置在错误的路径上——Planner定位局限性。没有与其他系统（如其他多智能体系统或先前工具如RepoFix、CodeGen with retrieval）进行比较，因为评估侧重于安全性和可部署性，而非原始解决率。通过安全控制堆栈隐式进行了消融实验（例如，复现基线失败以检测不稳定的CI）。

conclusion.en: The paper finds that Phoenix can safely resolve GitHub issues with zero regressions using multi-agent LLMs, but around half of pull requests suffer from planner localization errors—incorrect file paths—indicating that while safety controls are effective, localization accuracy remains a critical bottleneck. The baseline-aware test evaluation strategy ensures no test regressions but does not prevent these localization errors, a limitation compared to more rigorous validation like differential testing or formal verification. Moreover, the system's seven safety mechanisms were directly motivated by real-world deployment failure modes (e.g., WAF filtering, token expiry), highlighting how industrial constraints shape robust APR design. However, the evaluation used a curated 24-instance slice of SWE-bench Lite not comparable to the full benchmark, limiting generalizability, and the authors are addressing localization via retrieval-augmented generation.
conclusion.zh: 论文发现，Phoenix可以使用多智能体LLM安全地解决GitHub问题且无回归，但约一半的拉取请求存在Planner定位错误——错误的文件路径——表明尽管安全控制有效，定位准确性仍然是一个关键瓶颈。基线感知测试评估策略确保没有测试回归，但不能防止这些定位错误，与差分测试或形式化验证等更严格的验证相比，这是一个局限性。此外，系统的七项安全机制直接由真实世界的部署故障模式（如WAF过滤、令牌过期）驱动，强调了工业约束如何塑造鲁棒的APR设计。然而，评估使用了SWE-bench Lite的一个精心挑选的24实例子集，与完整基准不可比，限制了泛化性，并且作者正通过检索增强生成来解决定位问题。

question.en: How does Phoenix's baseline-aware test evaluation strategy specifically guard against overfitting patches, and what are its limitations compared to more rigorous validation techniques like differential testing or formal verification?
question.zh: Phoenix的基线感知测试评估策略具体如何防止补丁过拟合，与差分测试或形式化验证等更严格的验证技术相比，其局限性是什么？

reason.en: This question directly addresses the user's interest in avoiding overfitting patches. The paper describes a baseline test run before PR creation to ensure no pass-to-pass regressions, but this is a weak form of validation. Probing its effectiveness and limitations connects to the user's open question about detecting overfitting and integrating stronger validation methods.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接回应用户对避免补丁过拟合的兴趣。论文描述了在创建PR之前运行基线测试以确保没有通过到通过的回归，但这是一种较弱的验证形式。探究其有效性和局限性，与用户关于检测过拟合和整合更强验证方法的开放性问题相关。

question.en: What are the root causes of the planner agent's localization errors (incorrect file paths) in Phoenix, and how do these errors relate to the broader challenge of fault localization in LLM-driven APR systems?
question.zh: Phoenix中规划器智能体的定位错误（错误的文件路径）的根本原因是什么？这些错误与LLM驱动的APR系统中更广泛的故障定位挑战有何关联？

reason.en: The user is specifically interested in fault localization strategies for LLM-based repair. The paper reports that half of the PRs have incorrect paths due to planner limitations. This question probes the nature of those errors and their implications for improving localization, which is a core APR challenge.
reason.zh: 用户对基于LLM修复的故障定位策略特别感兴趣。论文报告称，由于规划器的局限性，一半的PR有错误的路径。这个问题探究了这些错误的性质及其对改进定位的意义，这是APR的核心挑战。

question.en: Given that Phoenix was evaluated on a curated 24-instance slice of SWE-bench Lite (not the full split), what are the key differences between this slice and the full benchmark that limit comparability, and what does this reveal about the representativeness of SWE-bench for real-world bugs?
question.zh: 鉴于Phoenix是在SWE-bench Lite的一个精心挑选的24个实例子集（而非完整分片）上评估的，该子集与完整基准之间的关键差异是什么限制了可比性？这对SWE-bench代表真实世界漏洞的程度有何启示？

reason.en: The user follows benchmark releases and is interested in how well they represent real-world bugs. The paper explicitly states the slice is not directly comparable to full-split leaderboard results. This question explores the selection bias and its implications for benchmark validity, aligning with the user's open question about Defects4J vs SWE-bench.
reason.zh: 用户关注基准发布，并对它们代表真实世界漏洞的程度感兴趣。论文明确说明该子集不能与完整分片的排行榜结果直接比较。这个问题探讨了选择偏差及其对基准有效性的影响，与用户关于Defects4J与SWE-bench的开放性问题一致。

question.en: What deployment failure modes (WAF filtering, token expiry, permission boundaries, flaky CI) motivated Phoenix's seven safety controls, and how do these industrial challenges inform the design of robust APR systems for real-world use?
question.zh: 哪些部署故障模式（WAF过滤、令牌过期、权限边界、不稳定的CI）促使了Phoenix的七项安全控制？这些工业挑战如何为面向真实世界使用场景的鲁棒APR系统设计提供信息？

reason.en: The user is interested in industrial deployment of APR. The paper lists specific failure modes that drove safety mechanisms. This question probes how real-world constraints shape system architecture, connecting to the user's open question about challenges in deploying APR in industrial settings.
reason.zh: 用户对APR的工业部署感兴趣。论文列出了驱动安全机制的具体故障模式。这个问题探究现实约束如何塑造系统架构，与用户关于在工业环境中部署APR的挑战的开放性问题相关。

question.en: How does Phoenix's baseline-aware test evaluation strategy specifically guard against overfitting patches, and what are its limitations compared to more rigorous validation techniques like differential testing or formal verification?
question.zh: Phoenix的基线感知测试评估策略具体如何防止补丁过拟合，与差分测试或形式化验证等更严格的验证技术相比，其局限性是什么？

reason.en: This question directly addresses the user's interest in avoiding overfitting patches. The paper describes a baseline test run before PR creation to ensure no pass-to-pass regressions, but this is a weak form of validation. Probing its effectiveness and limitations connects to the user's open question about detecting overfitting and integrating stronger validation methods.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接回应用户对避免补丁过拟合的兴趣。论文描述了在创建PR之前运行基线测试以确保没有通过到通过的回归，但这是一种较弱的验证形式。探究其有效性和局限性，与用户关于检测过拟合和整合更强验证方法的开放性问题相关。

answer.en: Phoenix's baseline-aware test evaluation guards against overfitting by running the existing test suite before PR creation and ensuring no pass-to-pass regressions, as stated: "Every change is checked against a baseline test run before a pull request is opened" and "no pass-to-pass regressions on successful runs." However, this strategy is limited because it only validates against the current test suite, not covering untested inputs or semantic correctness. The paper notes that "about half of the resulting pull requests are well-targeted fixes. The other half place code at incorrect paths," indicating that the baseline check does not prevent localization errors. Compared to differential testing (which systematically compares program outputs across versions) or formal verification (which proves correctness mathematically), Phoenix's method is weaker: it does not explore all possible behaviors nor provide proofs of correctness, and it misses issues that do not cause test failures.
answer.zh: Phoenix的基线感知测试评估通过在创建PR之前运行现有测试套件并确保没有通过到通过的回归来防止过拟合，如所述：“每个更改在打开拉取请求前都要经过基线测试运行检查”，并且“在成功运行中没有通过到通过的回归”。然而，这种策略有局限性，因为它仅针对当前测试套件进行验证，未覆盖未测试的输入或语义正确性。论文指出“约一半的拉取请求是定位准确的修复，另一半将代码放置在错误的路径上”，表明基线检查不能防止定位错误。与差分测试（系统性地比较不同版本的程序输出）或形式化验证（数学上证明正确性）相比，Phoenix的方法较弱：它不探索所有可能的行为，也不提供正确性证明，并且遗漏了不会导致测试失败的问题。

question.en: What are the root causes of the planner agent's localization errors (incorrect file paths) in Phoenix, and how do these errors relate to the broader challenge of fault localization in LLM-driven APR systems?
question.zh: Phoenix中规划器智能体的定位错误（错误的文件路径）的根本原因是什么？这些错误与LLM驱动的APR系统中更广泛的故障定位挑战有何关联？

reason.en: The user is specifically interested in fault localization strategies for LLM-based repair. The paper reports that half of the PRs have incorrect paths due to planner limitations. This question probes the nature of those errors and their implications for improving localization, which is a core APR challenge.
reason.zh: 用户对基于LLM修复的故障定位策略特别感兴趣。论文报告称，由于规划器的局限性，一半的PR有错误的路径。这个问题探究了这些错误的性质及其对改进定位的意义，这是APR的核心挑战。

answer.en: The Phoenix paper explicitly identifies the root cause of planner agent localization errors as a "planner localization limitation" that the authors are "addressing with retrieval." It states that about half of the pull requests "place code at incorrect paths" due to this limitation, and characterizes these as "incorrect paths" resulting from "planner limitations." The paper does not detail the specific mechanisms behind these errors (e.g., whether they stem from insufficient project context, flawed path reasoning, or incomplete repository structure understanding), and it does not further analyze how these errors relate to broader fault localization challenges in LLM-driven APR systems. The only corrective measure mentioned is an ongoing shift to retrieval-based localization, but no evaluation or analysis of that approach is provided.
answer.zh: Phoenix论文明确将规划器智能体定位错误的根本原因归为“规划器定位局限性”，作者正“用检索来解决”。它指出约一半的拉取请求由于这一局限性“将代码放置在错误的路径上”，并将其定性为“规划器局限性”导致的“错误路径”。论文未详述这些错误背后的具体机制（例如，是否源于项目上下文不足、路径推理有缺陷或仓库结构理解不完整），也未进一步分析这些错误与LLM驱动的APR系统中更广泛的故障定位挑战之间的关系。提到的唯一纠正措施是转向基于检索的定位，但未提供对该方法的评估或分析。

question.en: Given that Phoenix was evaluated on a curated 24-instance slice of SWE-bench Lite (not the full split), what are the key differences between this slice and the full benchmark that limit comparability, and what does this reveal about the representativeness of SWE-bench for real-world bugs?
question.zh: 鉴于Phoenix是在SWE-bench Lite的一个精心挑选的24个实例子集（而非完整分片）上评估的，该子集与完整基准之间的关键差异是什么限制了可比性？这对SWE-bench代表真实世界漏洞的程度有何启示？

reason.en: The user follows benchmark releases and is interested in how well they represent real-world bugs. The paper explicitly states the slice is not directly comparable to full-split leaderboard results. This question explores the selection bias and its implications for benchmark validity, aligning with the user's open question about Defects4J vs SWE-bench.
reason.zh: 用户关注基准发布，并对它们代表真实世界漏洞的程度感兴趣。论文明确说明该子集不能与完整分片的排行榜结果直接比较。这个问题探讨了选择偏差及其对基准有效性的影响，与用户关于Defects4J与SWE-bench的开放性问题一致。

answer.en: The paper explicitly states that the 24-instance slice is "curated" and "not directly comparable to full-split leaderboard results," but it does not enumerate the specific differences in selection criteria, difficulty distribution, or evaluation setup beyond noting it was "run on the production webhook path." Without details on how the slice was curated (e.g., whether easier instances were chosen or specific bug types were favored), the key differences that limit comparability remain unspecified. Consequently, the paper offers no direct evidence about the representativeness of SWE-bench for real-world bugs; it only acknowledges that the slice's results cannot be compared to the full benchmark, leaving the question of benchmark validity unaddressed.
answer.zh: 论文明确说明24个实例子集是“精心挑选的”，并且“不能与完整分片的排行榜结果直接比较”，但除了指出是“在生产webhook路径上运行”之外，并未列举选择标准、难度分布或评估设置方面的具体差异。没有关于子集如何精选的细节（例如，是否选择了较容易的实例或偏爱特定类型的漏洞），因此限制可比性的关键差异仍未明确。因此，论文未提供关于SWE-bench代表真实世界漏洞的直接证据；它仅承认子集的结果不能与完整基准比较，留下了基准有效性的问题未解决。

question.en: What deployment failure modes (WAF filtering, token expiry, permission boundaries, flaky CI) motivated Phoenix's seven safety controls, and how do these industrial challenges inform the design of robust APR systems for real-world use?
question.zh: 哪些部署故障模式（WAF过滤、令牌过期、权限边界、不稳定的CI）促使了Phoenix的七项安全控制？这些工业挑战如何为面向真实世界使用场景的鲁棒APR系统设计提供信息？

reason.en: The user is interested in industrial deployment of APR. The paper lists specific failure modes that drove safety mechanisms. This question probes how real-world constraints shape system architecture, connecting to the user's open question about challenges in deploying APR in industrial settings.
reason.zh: 用户对APR的工业部署感兴趣。论文列出了驱动安全机制的具体故障模式。这个问题探究现实约束如何塑造系统架构，与用户关于在工业环境中部署APR的挑战的开放性问题相关。

answer.en: The paper states that the four deployment failure modes—"WAF filtering, token expiry, permission boundaries, flaky CI"—directly motivated each of Phoenix's seven safety controls: "We also report the deployment failure modes (WAF filtering, token expiry, permission boundaries, flaky CI) that motivated each safety mechanism." These real-world constraints shaped Phoenix's architecture by requiring multiple layered safety guards to prevent code changes from breaking production systems. For example, the baseline-aware test evaluation strategy ensures "no pass-to-pass regressions on successful runs," addressing flaky CI, while the state machine and agent decomposition likely mitigate token expiry and permission boundaries by managing workflow continuity and access checks. The paper further notes that about half of the pull requests placed code at "incorrect paths," a localization limitation they are separately addressing, showing that deployment challenges exposed gaps in planner localization, which they aim to fix with retrieval augmentation. Thus, these industrial failure modes drove Phoenix to prioritize correctness preservation and regression avoidance, informing robust APR design by embedding safety controls directly into the multi-agent pipeline.
answer.zh: 论文指出，四种部署故障模式——“WAF过滤、令牌过期、权限边界、不稳定的CI”——直接促使了Phoenix的七项安全控制：“我们还报告了促使每项安全机制的部署故障模式（WAF过滤、令牌过期、权限边界、不稳定的CI）。”这些现实约束通过需要多层安全防护来防止代码更改破坏生产系统，塑造了Phoenix的架构。例如，基线感知测试评估策略确保“成功运行时无通过到通过的回归”，应对不稳定的CI，而状态机和智能体分解可能通过管理工作流连续性和访问检查来缓解令牌过期和权限边界。论文进一步指出，约一半的拉取请求将代码放置在“错误路径”上，这是他们正在单独解决的定位局限性，表明部署挑战暴露了规划器定位的不足，他们旨在通过检索增强来修复。因此，这些工业故障模式促使Phoenix优先考虑正确性保持和避免回归，通过将安全控制直接嵌入多智能体流水线来为鲁棒的APR设计提供信息。
